metadata.title: Guideline on the evaluation of medicinal products for cardiovascular disease prevention

rules.rule_text: An accurate definition of the CVD risk of the target population is fundamental when designing clinical trials for CV event prevention. Risk must be defined using integrated global risk scoring models or the presence of cardiovascular disease.
rules.source_section: 4.1 Patients characteristics and selection of patients
rules.verbatim_quote: when designing clinical trials for CVD event prevention, an accurate definition of the CVD risk of the target population is fundamental. There are two approaches for the definition of the target population at CVD risk: integrated global risk scoring models or CVD risk estimation based on the presence of cardiovascular disease.

rules.rule_text: The Applicant may be requested to justify the relevance of submitted data for EU populations, accounting for regional differences in risk profiles between high and low CV risk countries.
rules.source_section: 4.1 Patients characteristics and selection of patients
rules.verbatim_quote: the Applicant could be requested to justify the relevance of the submitted data for the EU populations taking into account that some integrated global risk scoring models (e.g. SCORE) have been adapted to countries with high and low cardiovascular risk.

rules.rule_text: The patient selection method must define a population with homogeneous and well-characterised risk levels. Mixing patients with significantly different absolute risk levels in the same trial is discouraged.
rules.source_section: 4.1 Patients characteristics and selection of patients
rules.verbatim_quote: the selection method should be adequate to define a population including patients with homogeneous and well-characterised risk levels, thus facilitating straightforward interpretation and applicability of the study results to the whole target population. ... Mixing in the same trial, patients with significant different absolute risk levels is discouraged, in particular where the magnitudes of the treatment effects are likely to differ according to absolute level of risk.
rules.exceptions: If clinically distinct subgroups are included, internal consistency investigations per ICH E9 are required.

rules.rule_text: CV prevention trials require long-term controlled, parallel, and preferably double-blind design. Treatment duration should usually last at least 12 months and preferably longer when intended use is lifelong (up to 5 years is reasonable).
rules.source_section: 4.2 Study design and duration of treatment
rules.verbatim_quote: According to the nature of the indication, long-term controlled, parallel and preferably double-blind clinical trials are generally necessary for both safety and efficacy. ... Treatment should usually last at least 12 months and preferably longer, notably when the intended use is lifelong. When the latter is the case, duration up to five years is reasonable.
rules.exceptions: For ACS patients, 6 months data may suffice for acute effects but at least 1 year for CV prevention.

rules.rule_text: Studies must be carried out on top of optimal drug treatment and lifestyle changes. Mechanisms to ensure optimal baseline therapy and control of CV risk factors must be implemented over the whole study period.
rules.source_section: 4.2 Study design and duration of treatment
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies have to be carried out on top of optimal drug treatment and lifestyle changes. It is crucial to implement mechanisms to ensure optimal baseline therapy and to control cardiovascular risk factors over the whole study period.

rules.rule_text: Predefined subgroup analyses are necessary for evaluation of safety and efficacy. Stratification of relevant subgroups should be considered.
rules.source_section: 4.2 Study design and duration of treatment
rules.verbatim_quote: Predefined subgroup analyses are necessary for the evaluation of safety and efficacy. Stratification of relevant subgroups should be considered.

rules.rule_text: A placebo-controlled study demonstrating superiority is the preferred approach when no established therapy exists for the specific target population.
rules.verbatim_quote: A placebo-controlled study aiming to demonstrate superiority provides an absolute estimate of the treatment effect. This is the preferred approach, if ethically acceptable, and if there is no established therapy for the specific target population or for a group of patients that is very similar.

rules.rule_text: For non-inferiority approach, the non-inferiority margin must be conservatively selected and properly justified in terms of clinical relevance. If more than one active comparator exists, only one is acceptable.
rules.verbatim_quote: When using the non-inferiority approach, establishing assay sensitivity is of paramount importance. ... The non-inferiority margin should be conservatively selected and properly justified in terms of its clinical relevance (CPMP/EWP/2158/99) in light of overall risks and benefits of each treatment. ... If there is more than one possible active comparator, only one of these comparators is acceptable.

rules.rule_text: Clinical outcome endpoints must be objective and clinically relevant. Objective CVD events need to be hospital-verified.
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical outcome endpoints should be objective and clinically relevant. In general, all-cause mortality and fatal CVD events are acceptable as primary endpoints. Usually, objective CVD events need to be hospital-verified.

rules.rule_text: All-cause mortality is preferred over cardiovascular mortality as the primary endpoint or as one component of the composite primary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: All-cause mortality is preferred over cardiovascular mortality as primary endpoint or as one component of the primary endpoint.
rules.exceptions: CV mortality acceptable if objectively and conservatively defined, may be more sensitive for non-inferiority approaches.

rules.rule_text: Composite endpoint components must have markedly similar weight in terms of clinical benefit. Including components with markedly different clinical weight (e.g., hospitalisation alongside fatal events) is discouraged.
rules.verbatim_quote: However, including in the composite, components which have a markedly different weight in term of clinical benefit is discouraged. An example is the combination in the primary composite endpoint of fatal events and clinician decision outcomes: hospitalisation, coronary revascularisation, amputation, use of rescue therapy, hospitalisation for heart failure.

rules.rule_text: Components of the composite endpoint should directly refer to the disease process and move in the same direction given effective treatment. The primary analysis should be based on time-to-first-event (survival) analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: The more clearly components of a composite endpoint directly refer to the disease process, the less there is any problem of interpretation. The more likely it is too that the components of the composite will move in the same direction given an effective treatment, which is important for sound interpretation. The primary analysis of a composite endpoint should be based on a 'time-to' first event (survival) analysis.

rules.rule_text: Analyses of each separate component of the composite must be presented. For overall mortality and CV mortality, both confidence intervals and point estimates are relevant. Any point estimate considerably in favour of the comparator is a matter of concern.
rules.verbatim_quote: To provide supportive information, and to ensure reliable interpretation, analyses of each separate component of the composite should be presented. For overall mortality and cardiovascular mortality both confidence intervals and point estimate are relevant for assessment. Any point estimate considerably in favour of the comparator is a matter of concern.

rules.rule_text: Events that are less objectively defined (e.g., TIA, silent MI, stable angina) require clinically relevant justification when used as components of a composite primary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: Other events, like transient ischemic attack, silent MI or stable angina pectoris are less likely to be objectively defined. Therefore, clinically relevant justifications should be provided when using them as components of a composite primary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Secondary outcome measures on which a claim is to be made must be embedded in the confirmatory testing strategy with appropriate Type I error control.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any secondary outcome measures on which a claim is to be made should be embedded into the confirmatory testing strategy appropriately controlling type I error (see CHMP Points to Consider on Multiplicity in Clinical Trials).

rules.rule_text: Surrogate marker validation for CV prevention requires demonstration of (1) biological plausibility, (2) correlation with epidemiological studies, and (3) treatment effects on the surrogate that predict treatment effects on outcome.
rules.verbatim_quote: Validation of surrogate markers relies on 3 basic principles with demonstration of (1) biological plausibility, (2) correlation with epidemiological studies and (3) treatment effects on the surrogate that predict treatment effects on outcome.

rules.rule_text: Neither overall mortality nor cardiovascular mortality should indicate a detrimental effect. For long-term treatment, long-term data on mortality and CV morbidity for at least 1 year are necessary as a rule.
rules.verbatim_quote: Neither overall mortality nor cardiovascular mortality should indicate a detrimental effect. If a long-term treatment is envisaged, long-term data on mortality and cardiovascular morbidity are necessary of as a rule at least 1 year.

rules.rule_text: Special attention must be drawn to inadvertent effects on parameters impacting the CV risk profile (blood pressure, lipid profile, glucose regulation) as well as bleeding and drug-drug interactions. At-risk subpopulations (elderly, renal, hepatic, cardiac failure) must be specifically considered.
rules.verbatim_quote: Special attention has to be drawn to possible inadvertent effects adherent to the study drug on parameters which may impact the cardio-vascular risk profile like blood pressure lowering effects, effects on lipid profile or glucose regulation. In addition bleeding or other relevant pharmacodynamic or pharmacokinetic drug-drug interactions should be monitored. Consideration should be given to all of the above-mentioned relevant subgroups and special patient populations at risk like the elderly, patients with renal, hepatic and cardiac failure.
